campbell.title: Campbell City Council — May 5, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to hear updates on local transportation planning from the regional transit authority and reviewed the city's finances for March 2026. The council also adopted a new policy restricting how the city's buildings and property can be used by federal agencies, and discussed efforts to hire and keep city employees.
keyTopics: Santa Clara Valley Transportation Authority presentation and transit planning
March 2026 investment and financial transactions report
New policy limiting federal agency use of city property
City job openings and employee recruitment/retention strategies

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — April 15, 2026
saratoga.summary: The city council held a meeting to address dangerous or unhealthy conditions on certain properties in Saratoga that the county had identified. They listened to any concerns residents had about cleaning up these problem properties, approved a plan to move forward with the cleanup work, and also discussed new rules about how the city handles technology-related issues and requests for accommodations from residents who need special assistance.
keyTopics: Public hearing on cleanup of hazardous properties identified by the county
City council approval to proceed with abatement work on problem properties
New technology disruption policy replacing the old one
Process for residents to request reasonable accommodations from the city

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — May 5, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on May 5, 2026, to approve meeting minutes and handle several maintenance and service agreements. The council declared overgrown brush and hazardous vegetation a public nuisance and approved a report for landscape and lighting maintenance. They also authorized the town manager to sign multi-year service contracts with vendors.
keyTopics: Hazardous brush and overgrown vegetation declared a public nuisance
Landscape and lighting maintenance report approved
Five-year service agreements authorized with Pacific (vendor name incomplete)
Second amendment to existing agreement approved

san-jose.title: San José City Council — May 13, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a meeting to organize upcoming agenda items and plan their schedule. They reviewed what should be included in their May 19th meeting, set time limits for certain discussions, and approved the meeting schedule for the next fiscal year.
keyTopics: Finalized the May 19, 2026 Council meeting agenda
Set time limits for specific agenda items to keep meetings on track
Approved the City Council meeting schedule for fiscal year 2026-2027
Assigned council members to various city committees and boards
Reviewed travel requests from council members

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — May 12, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The City Council held a private meeting to discuss two confidential matters: a potential lawsuit related to a water main problem at Cuesta Park, and negotiations to buy or lease two properties on Clyde Avenue. These closed-door discussions involved city lawyers and real estate staff working through sensitive issues that couldn't be discussed publicly.
keyTopics: Cuesta Park water main incident and possible legal claims
Property negotiations for 485 and 495 Clyde Avenue
Legal strategy discussion with city attorneys
Real estate negotiations led by city management

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — May 5, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The City Council held a special closed-door meeting at 5 P.M. to discuss confidential matters (the specific topics weren't disclosed to the public). They then reconvened for a regular public meeting at 7 P.M. with a flag salute to start.
keyTopics: Closed session meeting (details not publicly available)
Regular council meeting convened at 7 P.M.
Small business agenda item discussed

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — May 11, 2026
cupertino.summary: The City Council held an initial review meeting about Cupertino's budget plan for fiscal year 2026-27 to discuss how city money will be spent. The council also reminded residents and organizations about rules requiring anyone who tries to influence city decisions to register as a lobbyist and report their activities.
keyTopics: FY 2026-27 budget proposal review and discussion
Lobbyist registration requirements under City ordinance
Lobbying activity reporting rules for those influencing city decisions

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — May 11, 2026
santa-clara.summary: Santa Clara's City Council held a meeting on May 11, 2026 focused on economic development and marketing strategies for the city. The meeting was held in person but residents could also join remotely via Zoom or phone if they couldn't attend.
keyTopics: Economic Development & Marketing Committee (Ad Hoc) meetings
In-person and remote participation options via Zoom and phone
Public comment submission process (email by 11 AM meeting day)
Continued focus on promoting Santa Clara's business and growth

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — May 5, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to approve the city's spending plans for the next several years. Key decisions included finalizing a five-year capital improvement budget, reviewing the upcoming year's operating budget, and adopting a new master plan for parks and trails in the Innovation District.
keyTopics: 2027-2031 Capital Budget approval (5-year spending plan)
FY 2026-27 Operating Budget presentation and direction
Innovation District Parks and Trails Master Plan adoption
Updates to City Council meeting procedures and handbook
Approval of previous meeting minutes

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — May 7, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The city council held a hearing about a proposal to build a 6-story apartment building with 72 units at 450 Lytton Avenue, replacing a city parking lot. All 72 apartments would be permanently affordable for low-income residents, and the developer is asking for special allowances under state law to make the project work on the small lot.
keyTopics: 72 affordable housing units planned for 450 Lytton Avenue
6-story building replacing 52-space city parking lot
Request for density bonus waivers under California state law
Environmental review status under CEQA
